steps.title: Define the POV and scenario
steps.description: Describe who is holding the camera—such as an employee, fan, or insider—and what they are capturing in the leaked-style clip.

steps.title: Set tone, setting, and level of chaos
steps.description: Choose whether the footage feels like a subtle leak, high-energy backstage moment, or chaotic launch-day scene.

steps.title: Generate AI POV leaked footage
steps.description: Use AI to create handheld-feeling sequences with slight camera shake, imperfect framing, and natural motion appropriate for leaks.

steps.title: Export for teasers, countdowns, and viral posts
steps.description: Use the generated clips across TikTok, Reels, Shorts, and other social channels to build hype and anticipation.

items.question: What is the POV Leaked Footage workflow?
items.answer: POV Leaked Footage is an AI workflow for creating point-of-view "leaked" style videos that feel raw, behind-the-scenes, and shareable. It helps you simulate handheld, candid footage for teasers and viral campaigns.

items.question: Who is the POV Leaked Footage template designed for?
items.answer: POV Leaked Footage is useful for streetwear and fashion brands, music and entertainment marketers, tech and product launch teams, and agencies running social-first campaigns.

items.question: What input do I need to start?
items.answer: You describe what is being "leaked", from whose point of view, and the scenario—such as an employee, fan, or insider capturing a specific moment.

items.question: What type of output does this workflow produce?
items.answer: The workflow creates handheld-feeling POV video sequences with slight camera shake, imperfect framing, and natural motion that fit leaked-style teasers.

items.question: Where can I use POV leaked footage videos?
items.answer: You can use the clips across TikTok, Reels, Shorts, and social campaigns as teasers, countdown assets, and viral-style posts.

items.question: Does this workflow guarantee virality?
items.answer: No workflow can guarantee virality, but this template is designed to support formats commonly used in viral marketing and social-first launches.

items.question: How do credits and pricing work for POV Leaked Footage?
items.answer: Running AI video workflows in OpenCreator consumes credits depending on the models and clip lengths. Start with shorter tests and scale based on your campaign results.
